sq SqueezyAgent
How it works Docs Benchmarks
GitHub
local static engine / cost-first coding agent

Understand code structure before spending LLM tokens.

SqueezyAgent is a coding agent built to inspect your repository locally, find the right files and symbols, and send the model only the context it needs. Less blind search. Less repeated reading. Less wasted spend.

Follow on GitHub Explore the draft docs

squeezy plan --task impact-auth-change

$ squeezy ask "what changes if auth middleware moves?"
structure auth code connects to routes, sessions, and tests
evidence 6 code slices, 4 callers, 3 tests, reasons attached
budget raw search avoided; compact packet sent to model
next read exact slices only where patching or verification needs code
Navigation structure-first
Context only what matters
Reads targeted slices
Spend receipt-tracked
current state

Runnable foundation today. Code-structure engine next.

Main already has the TUI foundation, OpenAI Responses streaming, Anthropic Messages streaming, deterministic validation harness tasks in CI, and bounded fallback tools with receipts and output budgets.

providers

OpenAI and Anthropic streaming.

Provider tool calls feed structured tool outputs back into the model loop instead of bypassing Squeezy's runtime controls.

tools

Bounded fallback navigation exists.

Ignore-aware grep, path-only glob, capped reads, spill previews, aggregate result budgets, and permission-gated web tools are already part of the foundation.

proof

Deterministic harness before live spend.

CI runs mock OpenAI, mock Anthropic, and grep-baseline tasks without network access or model cost.

the problem

Agents should not burn tokens just to find the right file.

Many coding agents still navigate like a model holding search: list directories, grep broad text, read whole files, forget after compaction, then repeat. That loop is slow and expensive.

01 / wasted exploration

Search noise gets billed like useful reasoning.

Raw files, noisy shell output, and repeated reads become model context even when local analysis could have found the right slice.

02 / missing structure

Text search sees strings, not code relationships.

Real code work needs files, functions, modules, callers, tests, and dependencies. SqueezyAgent is built to find those locally first.

03 / spend control

Teams should own the agent runtime.

SqueezyAgent is designed to make cost visible and enforceable at the navigation layer, not only after the bill arrives.

tool-call savings foundation

The current runtime already refuses obvious waste.

Before the code-structure engine lands, SqueezyAgent already makes fallback tools cheaper and more accountable. That foundation is what the local engine will plug into.

search

Path, count, or content.

Grep can return matching lines, files with matches, or a count, so broad exploration does not always ship raw content to the model.

spill

Large outputs become handles.

Oversized tool results are stored locally and returned as compact previews with exact-range handles.

budget

One round has one budget.

Parallel tool calls are packed against an aggregate result budget instead of each call flooding the next prompt.

for teams

Control agent spend without surrendering the runtime.

SqueezyAgent is built for organizations and developers who want the capability of model-powered agents without letting provider incentives define every navigation decision.

spend visibility

Receipts before invoices.

Track files read, tool output, repeated context avoided, and model tokens as first-class runtime facts.

provider neutral

Bring your model. Keep the agent disciplined.

Routing and budgets belong in the agent runtime, not only in the provider billing dashboard.

local understanding

Spend CPU before tokens.

Build code structure locally, then ask the model to reason over compact evidence instead of raw repository noise.

the squeezy loop

Use local code analysis before paid model context.

The model should spend tokens on judgment. Your machine should spend CPU on finding files, relationships, receipts, and targeted reads before billable context enters the prompt.

input User intent

"Move auth middleware without breaking session tests."

local Static analysis

Parse files, symbols, modules, imports, calls, and tests.

graph Structure lookup

Find owners, dependencies, callers, tests, and likely impact.

packet Evidence packet

Return compact code slices, hashes, and reasons.

model Targeted context

Read slices only when editing or verifying needs text.

cost control

Every read should earn its place in context.

SqueezyAgent is built around receipts, budgets, deduped reads, compact tool output, and local code evidence. The goal is not smaller context for its own sake. The goal is better work with less spend.

receipt-backed context packet

path crates/agent/src/session.rs:114..188
reason owns compaction receipt replay
reason import path plus local references
receipt sha256:3b2... / unchanged ranges deduped
proof before claims

Honest until measured. Sharper when v0 lands.

This site is a draft surface for a product still being built. Public savings claims should come from real receipts, benchmark traces, and repeatable correctness checks.

Benchmark method Read the docs
sq SqueezyAgent

A local static engine helps the agent understand code structure before wasting LLM tokens on search.

Docs Benchmarks Contact GitHub
